zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joanna Whitton於1330年出生在英國薩默塞特文卡頓時，她的父親Sir John Whyton31歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Fitzjohn31歲。她於1347年與Sir Edmund Hussey在英國英格蘭薩默塞特文卡頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1411年3月21日在英國戴文可雷迪頓享年81歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特法爾 格福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joanna Whitton于1330年出生在英国薩默塞特文卡頓时，她的父亲Sir John Whyton31岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Fitzjohn31岁。她于1347年与Sir Edmund Hussey在英国英格兰薩默塞特文卡頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1411年3月21日在英国戴文可雷迪頓享年81岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特法爾 格福德。

nameMeanings.name: Whitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Whitton in Morebattle (Roxburghshire). The placename is from Old English hwīt ‘white’ + tūn ‘farm, village’, as with many of the places in 2 below.

English: habitational name from any of the places called Whitton (Durham, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Northumberland, Shropshire, Suffolk) or Witton (Cheshire, Durham, Lancashire, Herefordshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Warwickshire, Worcestershire, North Yorkshire); or possibly from Wyton (Huntingdonshire, East Yorkshire); or possibly from Weeton, Market Weighton, or Little Weighton (East Yorkshire). The placenames do not all share the same etymology but most of their post-medieval spellings are interchangeable. Most of the placenames derive from the Old English personal name Hwīta or Old English hwīt ‘white’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some, for example Witton in Warwickshire, derive from Old English wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farmstead’ + tūn, and some others, for example Witton in Norfolk, derive from Old English widu ‘wood’ + tūn. Whitton in Lincolnshire derives from the Old English personal name Hwīta (genitive Hwītan) or Old English hwīt (dative hwītan) + ēg ‘island’. Witton in Lancashire derives from the Old English personal Witta + Old English tūn.
English: in Devon, this surname is a variant of   Whiddon  .

variants.name: Whitten
meanings: English: variant of   Whitton   or   Whiting  .

variants.name: Whetten
meanings: English, Scottish, or northern Irish: variant of   Whitton  .

variants.name: Whiddon
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from any of numerous minor places in Devon named Whiddon. Some are named with Old English hwīt ‘white’ + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’; others with Old English hwǣte ‘wheat’ + dūn or denu ‘valley’.

variants.name: Whiton
meanings: English (Essex): variant of   Whitton  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
History: James Whiton of Hingham, Norfolk, England, came to Plymouth, MA, in 1635.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joanna Whitton was born in 1330, in Wincanton, Somerset, England, her father, Sir John Whyton, was 31 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth Fitzjohn, was 31. She married Sir Edmund Hussey in 1347, in Wincanton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 21 March 1411, in Crediton, Devon, England, at the age of 81, and was buried in Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée le 21 mars 1411, à Crediton, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterrée à Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Whitton(이)가 1330년 영국 서머세트 윈캔턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Whyton(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Fitzjohn(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1347년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 윈캔턴에서 Sir Edmund Hussey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1411년 3월 21일 영국 데폰 크레딧에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 패를리 헌그르퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joanna Whitton родилась в 1330 г. в местности винкантон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Whyton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Fitzjohn, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edmund Hussey в 1347 г. в местности винкантон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 марта 1411 г. в местности кредитон, девон, Англия в возрасте 81 год и была похоронена в местности фарлиг хунгирфорд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.
